lede: A look at July 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Elvina Kalieva and Lorik Cana, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2015 Gurdaspur attack
events.desc: Seven people are killed and many injured after gunmen attack an Indian police station in Punjab.

events.title: Jamaat al Muslimeen
events.desc: The Jamaat al Muslimeen attempt a coup d'état in Trinidad and Tobago.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: Five thousand more American military advisers are sent to South Vietnam bringing the total number of United States forces in Vietnam to 21,000.

events.title: Austrian State Treaty
events.desc: The Austrian State Treaty restores Austrian sovereignty.

events.title: El Al Flight 402
events.desc: El Al Flight 402 is shot down by two fighter jets after straying into Bulgarian air space. All 58 people on board are killed.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Allied forces successfully halt the final Axis advance into Egypt.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Allies reach the Yser Canal at the Battle of Passchendaele.

events.title: Wilhelm II
events.desc: Kaiser Wilhelm II makes a speech comparing Germans to Huns; for years afterwards, "Hun" would be a disparaging name for Germans.

events.title: Army Medical Department (United States)
events.desc: Founding of the U.S. Army Medical Department: The Second Continental Congress passes legislation establishing "an hospital for an army consisting of 20,000 men."

events.title: Siward, Earl of Northumbria
events.desc: Siward, Earl of Northumbria, invades Scotland and defeats Macbeth, King of Scotland, somewhere north of the Firth of Forth. This is known as the Battle of Dunsinane.

births.name: Gorden Tallis
births.line: Australian rugby league player and coach

births.name: Shirley Williams
births.line: English academic and politician, Secretary of State for Education (died 2021)

births.name: José Celso Barbosa
births.line: Puerto Rican physician, sociologist, and politician (died 1921)

deaths.text: Edna O'Brien, Irish novelist, playwright, poet and short story writer
Lindy Boggs, American politician and diplomat, 5th United States Ambassador to the Holy See
Bob Hope, English-American actor, comedian, television personality, and businessman
Smoky Joe Wood, American baseball player and coach
Julie Vinter Hansen, Danish-Swiss astronomer and academic

observances.text: Christian feast day: Aurelius and Natalia and companions of the Martyrs of Córdoba.
Christian feast day: Maurus, Pantalemon, and Sergius
Christian feast day: Seven Sleepers of Ephesus (Roman Martyrology) National Sleepy Head Day (Finland)
